title: Ordinary Differential Equations and Initial Values · Stakeholder Model Mission
summary: A community microgrid planning team. The team needs a calibrated dynamic storage model with unit, solver, and uncertainty evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Energy Demand and Storage Dynamics with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate identifiability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Communicate numerical ivp in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Communicate least squares in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A model passes through (4, 14) and (8, 26). Calculate its average rate of change.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.
